Bld
Docs Releases GitHub
Get Started
v0.4.0 · Now Available

Simple and blazingly fast CI/CD

Run pipelines locally, in Docker containers, or on remote servers. Write YAML, execute anywhere. Written in Rust.

Get Started View on GitHub
terminal

# initialize a project

$ bld init

# run a pipeline

$ bld run -p build.yaml

Everything you need to automate your pipelines

Powerful features with a simple YAML interface.

Run Anywhere

Execute pipelines on your local machine, inside Docker containers, or on remote servers via SSH. One tool, every environment.

Docker Native

Pull existing images, build from Dockerfiles, and connect to private registries including Docker Hub and GHCR.

Parallel Jobs

Version 2 pipelines support named jobs that run concurrently. Define sequential or parallel execution — you decide.

Server Mode

Deploy bld as a dedicated server for team use. Monitor runs, manage pipelines, and view full history in the built-in Web UI.

Cron Scheduling

Schedule pipelines to run automatically using cron expressions. Server-managed with complete visibility and control.

GitHub Action

Official bld-action available on the GitHub Marketplace. Integrate bld pipelines seamlessly into any GitHub workflow.

Write pipelines in YAML.
Run them anywhere.

Pipeline syntax is versioned independently from the tool itself. Upgrade at your own pace — existing pipelines keep working.

  • Version 2 with parallel job support
  • Variables and environment configuration
  • Artifact management between environments
  • Cron scheduling built in
Read the pipeline docs 
name: build-and-test
version: 2

runs_on:
  image: node:lts
  pull: true

variables:
  branch: main

artifacts:
  - method: get
    from: /app/dist
    to: ./dist
    after: test

jobs:
  install:
    - working_dir: /app
      exec:
        - git clone -b ${{branch}} https://github.com/your-org/app .
        - npm install

  test:
    needs:
      - install
    - working_dir: /app
      exec:
        - npm run test
        - npm run build

Up and running in minutes

Three steps from zero to your first pipeline.

01

Install

Download the latest prebuilt binary from GitHub Releases. Supports Linux (musl) and Windows (mingw).

$ # github.com/Kani-Maki-Gang/bld/releases
02

Initialize

Run bld init in any directory to create a .bld/ folder with a config file and your first pipeline ready to go.

$ bld init
03

Run

Create YAML pipeline files, define jobs and steps, then execute locally, in Docker, or push to a remote bld server.

$ bld run -p build.yaml

Integrate with GitHub Actions

Use the official bld-github-action to trigger bld pipelines directly from your GitHub workflows. Available on the GitHub Marketplace.

View on GitHub 
name: CI
on: [push]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: Kani-Maki-Gang/bld-github-action@v1
        with:
          server: ${{ secrets.BLD_SERVER }}
          token: ${{ secrets.BLD_TOKEN }}
          pipeline: build.yaml
bld

Simple and blazingly fast CI/CD.
Run pipelines anywhere.
